posts.name: Brian S t o t t e r
posts.cooked:
• 2019 Morgen Long Chardonnay X Omni - USA, Oregon, Willamette Valley, Eola - Amity Hills (6/2/2021)
    Decanted for 2 hours before having my first taste, and continued to follow over the evening. Consumed from a Grassl Cru glass.

This is a riesling lover’s chardonnay right here. Beautifully complex nose of key lime pie, orange zest, custard, almond meal, wood spice, and steel. The wine is firm on the palate and has excellent tension, with electric acidity, and great dry extract. Some oak presence but well integrated. Persistent lime and orange cream citrus notes linger over a bed of chalky minerals, which I’m still tasting minutes after my last sip.

Superb juice. In a word, electric! (95 pts.)

1024×768 167 KB

Posted from CellarTracker

Damn, that sounds delicious!! Just an incredible note Brian - I’ll have to look for this!

You may have to order from Seth directly. I know some local OR wine shops have his wines from the past couple vintages, not sure about this one. Each of his 2019s I’ve tried so far have been excellent.

posts.name: Todd Hamina
posts.cooked:
I think you’d be pretty hard pressed to find a domestic producer so entirely off the rails keen on making world class Chardonnay than Seth. You Chard lovers should be ringing his phone while he still has wines available.


Disclaimer: none.
